title: Mandatory email notification channel
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want email to be available as a mandatory, always-on notification channel, so that all critical HRMS communications reliably reach every recipient regardless of other channel availability.
acceptance_criteria: Given the notification system is provisioned for a company, when channels are configured, then email is always enabled and cannot be disabled or removed as a channel.; Given an event triggers a notification, when no other channel is selected or available, then the notification is delivered via email by default.; Given a recipient (including an Employee (Non-User)) has no in-app or portal access, when an event targets them, then email is used as the fallback delivery channel.; Given a user attempts to opt out of email in their preferences, when saving, then email remains enforced for mandatory/critical communications.

title: Optional in-app notifications
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want to receive in-app notifications within the HRMS when the company enables them, so that I can see relevant updates without leaving the application.
acceptance_criteria: Given in-app notifications are enabled for the company, when an event targets me, then I see the notification inside the application in real time.; Given in-app notifications are optional, when the company has not enabled them, then no in-app notifications are shown and other enabled channels are used.; Given I have unread in-app notifications, when I open the notification area, then I can view them and they reflect the correct event context.; Given in-app is enabled alongside email, when an event fires, then delivery respects the recipient's channel preferences subject to mandatory email.

title: Microsoft Teams and WhatsApp connectors
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to optionally enable Microsoft Teams and WhatsApp delivery via third-party connectors, so that employees can receive notifications on the messaging tools they already use.
acceptance_criteria: Given a third-party connector for Teams or WhatsApp is configured and available, when a notification event fires, then the notification can be delivered through that connector.; Given these channels are optional, when no connector is configured, then Teams/WhatsApp delivery is unavailable and other enabled channels (with email as fallback) are used.; Given a connector is enabled, when I choose it for an event type, then notifications route through the connector in addition to or instead of other optional channels while email remains mandatory.; Given a connector becomes unavailable or fails, when delivery is attempted, then the system falls back to email so the notification is not lost.

title: Approval notifications
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to receive approval notifications, so that I am promptly informed when items require my approval and can act without delay.
acceptance_criteria: Given an item requires my approval, when it is submitted, then I receive an approval notification through my configured channels with email guaranteed.; Given an approval notification is sent, when I open it, then it identifies the item, the requester, and the action awaiting me.; Given an approval event, when the notification is generated, then it is dispatched event-driven (immediately) rather than waiting for a batch.; Given multiple approvals are pending, when a digest schedule applies, then approval notifications may also be consolidated into a digest per user preference.

title: Escalation alerts
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to receive escalation alerts, so that overdue or unattended items are surfaced to the right person before they are missed.
acceptance_criteria: Given an item meets its escalation condition, when the escalation triggers, then an escalation alert is sent to the appropriate recipient(s).; Given an escalation alert is delivered, when it is received, then it indicates the item, the reason for escalation, and the required action.; Given an item remains unattended after escalation, when the next escalation threshold is met, then a further escalation alert is dispatched to the next recipient in the chain.; Given escalation alerts, when they fire, then they are delivered event-driven so overdue items are surfaced in real time.

title: Workflow status change notifications
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want to be notified when the status of a workflow I am involved in changes, so that I stay aware of progress and outcomes.
acceptance_criteria: Given a workflow status changes, when the change is committed, then all affected participants receive a status change notification through their configured channels.; Given a status change notification is received, when I read it, then it reflects the new workflow status and the item it relates to.; Given I am not a participant in a workflow, when its status changes, then I do not receive a notification for it.; Given I have subscribed to workflow status events, when a change occurs, then delivery respects my channel and frequency preferences.

title: Reminders and lifecycle event notifications
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want to receive reminders and lifecycle event notifications, so that I do not miss pending actions or key employment events.
acceptance_criteria: Given a reminder condition is met, when it triggers, then a reminder notification is delivered to the relevant recipient through their configured channels.; Given a lifecycle event occurs (e.g., onboarding, role change, exit), when it is recorded, then a corresponding lifecycle event notification is sent to the affected parties.; Given a reminder relates to a pending action, when I receive it, then it identifies the action and its due context.; Given reminders can recur, when the pending action is still outstanding, then subsequent reminders may be sent per the configured schedule.

title: Event-driven delivery
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want the platform to deliver notifications in an event-driven manner, so that recipients are informed in real time as events occur.
acceptance_criteria: Given a notification-triggering event occurs, when it is processed, then the corresponding notification is generated and dispatched immediately.; Given event-driven delivery, when an event fires, then delivery does not wait for a scheduled batch or digest.; Given a delivery attempt fails on the selected channel, when event-driven dispatch runs, then the system retries or falls back to email so the notification is delivered.; Given high event volume, when many events fire, then each is processed and dispatched without blocking others.

title: Scheduler-driven digests and summaries
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want to receive scheduler-driven digests and summaries, so that I can consolidate multiple updates instead of receiving them individually.
acceptance_criteria: Given a digest schedule is configured, when the scheduled time arrives, then accumulated notifications are compiled and delivered as a single digest or summary.; Given scheduler-driven delivery, when the schedule runs, then it aggregates the relevant events for the period since the last digest.; Given no events occurred in the period, when the schedule runs, then no empty digest is sent (or an empty-state summary per configuration).; Given I have set a delivery frequency, when the scheduler runs, then digests honor my frequency preference.

title: Configurable branded templates
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want configurable notification templates with company branding, so that communications present a consistent, professional identity.
acceptance_criteria: Given a notification template, when I edit it, then I can apply company branding (logo, colors, styling) and save the configuration.; Given branding is configured, when a notification is sent using the template, then the delivered message reflects the company branding.; Given multiple event types, when I configure templates, then each event type can have its own branded template.; Given a template is updated, when new notifications are generated, then they use the latest saved template version.

title: Template placeholders and dynamic data insertion
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want templates to support placeholders and dynamic data insertion, so that each notification is populated with the correct context-specific details.
acceptance_criteria: Given a template contains placeholders, when a notification is generated, then the placeholders are replaced with the corresponding dynamic data (e.g., name, item, date).; Given a placeholder has no available value, when the notification is rendered, then the template handles the missing value gracefully without breaking delivery.; Given I edit a template, when I insert a placeholder, then I can select from the supported dynamic fields for that event type.; Given dynamic data insertion, when the notification is delivered across any channel, then the resolved values render consistently.
source: FR 6.27.4 (Templating — placeholders and dynamic data insertion)

title: User notification preferences
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want to configure my channel preferences, event subscriptions, and delivery frequency, so that I receive notifications the way I prefer.
acceptance_criteria: Given my notification preferences, when I select preferred channels, then future notifications respect my channel selections subject to mandatory email.; Given event subscription settings, when I subscribe to or unsubscribe from an event type, then I only receive notifications for my subscribed events (except mandatory/critical events).; Given frequency configuration, when I set a delivery frequency (e.g., immediate, daily digest, weekly), then notifications are delivered according to that frequency.; Given I attempt to disable email for critical communications, when I save, then email remains enforced for those events.

title: Configure third-party notification connectors
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want to provision and manage the Microsoft Teams and WhatsApp third-party connectors, so that these optional messaging channels can be made available for companies to use.
acceptance_criteria: Given the platform, when I configure a Teams or WhatsApp connector, then I can supply the integration credentials and enable the connector for use.; Given a connector is provisioned, when a Company Admin enables the channel, then notifications can be routed through it.; Given a connector is disabled or its credentials are invalid, when delivery is attempted, then the platform reports the failure and falls back to email.; Given optional channels, when no connector is provisioned, then Teams/WhatsApp remain unavailable and email plus other enabled channels are used.

title: Email notifications for non-user employees
user_story: As an Employee (Non-User), I want to receive lifecycle and event notifications by email, so that I stay informed about my employment events even though I do not have HRMS application access.
acceptance_criteria: Given I have no in-app or portal access, when an event or lifecycle notification targets me, then it is delivered to my email address as the mandatory channel.; Given optional channels require app access, when a notification is generated for me, then in-app delivery is not attempted and email is used.; Given a template with placeholders, when my notification is rendered, then my context-specific details are inserted correctly for email delivery.; Given a lifecycle event (e.g., onboarding or exit), when it is recorded, then a corresponding email notification is sent to me.
source: FR 6.27.1, FR 6.27.2 (Channels/Events — email delivery for non-user employees)

title: Group-level branded templates and notification configuration
user_story: As a Group Company Admin, I want to configure branded notification templates and channel settings across the companies in my group, so that communications are consistent within the group while allowing company-level specialization.
acceptance_criteria: Given a group of companies, when I configure a template or branding at the group level, then it can serve as the default for the group's companies.; Given a company overrides a group template, when a notification is sent for that company, then the company-specific template takes precedence.; Given group-level channel settings, when I enable or disable optional channels, then the setting applies to the group's companies subject to mandatory email.; Given placeholders and dynamic data, when a group template is used, then values resolve correctly per the target company.

title: Portfolio-wide notification oversight and defaults
user_story: As a Portfolio Admin, I want to set default notification channels, templates, and event configurations across my portfolio, so that all group companies start from consistent communication standards.
acceptance_criteria: Given a portfolio of group companies, when I define default channel and template settings, then they cascade as defaults to the group companies in the portfolio.; Given a group or company customizes settings, when a notification is generated, then the more specific configuration overrides the portfolio default.; Given mandatory email, when I configure portfolio defaults, then email cannot be disabled at any level.; Given portfolio oversight, when I review notification configuration, then I can see which channels and templates are in effect across the portfolio.
source: FR 6.27.1, FR 6.27.4, FR 6.27.5 (Portfolio-level notification defaults and oversight)

title: Configure digest schedules and event delivery models
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to configure which events are event-driven versus batched into scheduler-driven digests and set the digest schedules, so that employees receive the right balance of real-time and consolidated communications.
acceptance_criteria: Given event types, when I configure delivery, then I can designate each as event-driven (immediate) or included in a scheduler-driven digest.; Given a digest, when I set its schedule (e.g., daily or weekly at a chosen time), then the scheduler compiles and delivers accumulated notifications at that time.; Given critical events such as approvals and escalations, when I configure delivery, then they remain event-driven and are not suppressed by digest batching.; Given a user has a personal frequency preference, when the company digest schedule runs, then the user preference is respected within the allowed options.
source: FR 6.27.3, FR 6.27.2 (Delivery Models — event-driven vs scheduler-driven configuration)

title: Persist notification and per-channel delivery records
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want every generated notification and its per-channel delivery outcome persisted as auditable records, so that delivery can be traced, retried, and reported on.
acceptance_criteria: Given a notification is generated, when it is dispatched, then a notification record and per-channel delivery-attempt record (channel, recipient, status, timestamp) are persisted.; Given a delivery succeeds or fails, when the outcome is known, then the corresponding record is updated with the final status and any error reason.; Given an audit or investigation, when records are queried, then the full delivery history for a given event or recipient is retrievable.; Given records are historical, when past deliveries are reviewed, then their captured content and status are immutable and reflect exactly what was sent at the time.
source: FR 6.27.2, FR 6.27.3 (L1 data — notification and delivery records)

title: Tenant-scoped isolation of notification data
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want notification records, templates, and user preferences stored under tenant-scoped isolation (RLS), so that no company can access another company's communications or configuration.
acceptance_criteria: Given multi-tenant storage, when any notification data is persisted, then each record is tagged with its owning tenant/company and protected by row-level security.; Given a user or admin queries notifications or preferences, when the query runs, then only records within their tenant scope are returned.; Given a connector or background job reads configuration, when it executes, then it cannot access another tenant's templates, preferences, or delivery logs.; Given the group/portfolio hierarchy, when scoped access applies, then visibility follows the hierarchy without leaking peer-company data.

title: Versioned and effective-dated templates and preferences
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want templates and notification preferences stored as versioned, effective-dated records, so that changes are tracked and the correct version applies at send time.
acceptance_criteria: Given a template is edited, when I save, then a new version is persisted with effective dating while prior versions are retained in history.; Given a notification is generated, when the engine resolves the template, then it uses the template version effective as of the send time.; Given a preference change, when it is saved, then the change is recorded with its effective date and the prior settings are preserved.; Given an audit, when I review a past notification, then I can determine which template version and which preferences were in effect when it was sent.
source: FR 6.27.4, FR 6.27.5 (L1 data — versioned/effective-dated config records)

title: Notification engine resolves recipients channels and templates at dispatch
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want the shared Notification/Template engine to interpret per-tenant config at dispatch — resolving recipients, channels, and templates — so that notifications are produced consistently across all modules.
acceptance_criteria: Given an event is raised by any module, when the notification engine processes it, then it reads the tenant's channel config, template, and recipient rules to construct the message.; Given multiple channels are enabled, when the engine dispatches, then it routes to each enabled channel while enforcing mandatory email.; Given the resolved template contains placeholders, when the engine renders, then it substitutes dynamic event data and produces channel-appropriate output.; Given the engine is shared, when different modules raise events, then the same engine logic applies uniformly without module-specific code.

title: Engine delivery guarantee with retry fallback and dead-letter
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want the notification engine to guarantee delivery through retry, email fallback, and dead-letter handling, so that notifications are not silently lost.
acceptance_criteria: Given a channel delivery fails, when the engine detects the failure, then it retries per a configured backoff policy before failing over.; Given a non-email channel remains unavailable, when retries are exhausted, then the engine falls back to email.; Given all delivery attempts fail, when the engine gives up, then the notification is moved to a dead-letter store and flagged for admin review.; Given high event volume, when the engine processes the queue, then a failure on one notification does not block delivery of others.
source: FR 6.27.3 (L3 engine — delivery guarantee / retry / dead-letter)

title: In-app notification center screen
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want an in-app notification center in the SPA where I can view, filter, and manage my notifications, so that I can track and act on updates from one place.
acceptance_criteria: Given in-app notifications are enabled, when I open the notification center, then I see my notifications with read/unread state, timestamp, and event context.; Given unread notifications, when I open or mark them read, then the unread count and indicator update accordingly.; Given a notification references an item, when I select it, then I am navigated to the relevant screen or record for that item.; Given many notifications, when I filter or search by event type or date, then the list updates to match.
source: FR 6.27.1, FR 6.27.2 (L4 presentation — in-app notification center)

title: Template editor screen with live preview
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want a template editor screen in the SPA with branding controls, placeholder insertion, and live preview, so that I can author notification templates without technical help.
acceptance_criteria: Given the template editor, when I edit a template, then I can apply branding, insert supported placeholders from a picker, and see a live preview rendered with sample data.; Given a placeholder is inserted, when I preview, then the preview renders resolved sample values for that event type.; Given I save the template, when validation passes, then the template is stored and made available to the engine, and if validation fails then errors are shown inline.; Given per-event templates, when I select an event type, then the editor loads that event's template for editing.
